title: Should You Buy GTBIF Stock After Marijuana Reclassification Order?
text: The key regulatory development for cannabis stocks relates to rescheduling rather than full legalization. Reports indicated that the federal government might reclassify marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III under the Controlled Substances Act. Section 280E only applies to Schedule I and II substances, so moving cannabis to Schedule III would eliminate the tax burden overnight. Green Thumb Industries generated $195 million in cash flow from operations in 2024—but only after paying $131 million in taxes. If that tax burden vanishes due to rescheduling, those numbers would improve dramatically. Analysts covering GTBIF expect its earnings to grow by 115.7% to $0.32 per share in 2024, further rising by 6.9% in 2025. On Wall Street, Green Thumb stock is a strong buy, with eight out of 10 analysts rating it a strong buy and two calling it a moderate buy, with an average target price of $21.34 implying a potential 127.3% increase in the next 12 months. Green Thumb Industries received a price target raise to $10 from $8 at Benchmark on May 7, 2026, and the price target was also raised to C$25 from C$21 at Alliance Global on May 7, 2026.
